gaps.issue: Fragmented font-size hierarchy: 9+ distinct sizes (0.7rem through 1rem) without a clear typographic scale. Sizes like 0.8125rem and 0.8rem are arbitrary.
Several secondary UI elements below 14px minimum: buttons (0.75rem/12px), status bar (0.75rem/12px), tool error text (0.75rem/12px), code blocks (0.85rem/13.6px).

Hardcoded rgba/hex colors throughout MessageCard.vue for error states (#ff6b6b, #ffa500), tool states, hover effects, and borders instead of theme variables. Missing --error-bg, --warning, --success variables in theme.css.
Border-radius uses 5+ values (0, 4px, 6px, 8px, 50%) without a standardized tier system.
Transition durations inconsistent: 0.15s, 0.2s, 0.3s across different elements without clear rationale for tiers.
Tool round badge uses 0.2rem vertical padding (3.2px), off the 8px grid.
No timestamps on messages — conversation lacks temporal anchoring for a real chat feel. Requires data model changes, deferred.
gaps.note: Deferred — requires data model changes beyond CSS scope

criticalIssues: Body text below WCAG AA minimum (15px)

afterIteration3.remainingIssues: Typography hierarchy fragmentation, hardcoded colors, and minor consistency gaps — all outside iteration 3 scope

qualityAssessment.improvement: +12.8 points — all critical accessibility and visual hierarchy issues resolved

rationale: Gap score 17.4 is below the 20 threshold. All 8 design-3 CSS changes verified as correctly implemented. The three critical issues from iteration 2 (sub-WCAG body text, indistinguishable user/assistant messages, off-grid spacing) are fully resolved. Remaining gaps are design system maturity concerns (color variable centralization, typographic scale consolidation, border-radius/transition standardization) that represent future polish work, not blockers to the Notion-style conversation goal.
